name: Swiss Steak
recipeInstructions:
1. Rub the steak with minced garlic and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
2. Pour 1/2 cup of flour onto one side of the meat and pound it in using a meat mallet or the edge of a heavy saucer. Turn the steak over and repeat on the other side.
3. Heat oil in a large frying pan and brown the steak on both sides.
4. Add sliced celery, quartered carrots, and sliced onion to the pan. Cook until vegetables are tender.
5. Stir in 16 cups of tomato sauce and season with salt and pepper to taste.
6. Cover the pan tightly and simmer over low heat for 1 1/2 hours or until the meat is tender.
7. Cut the steak into serving-sized pieces and serve with gravy from the pan. Good with noodles, rice, or mashed potatoes.
